hyperedges.label: Monitoring Stack Components and Data Flow
VPN-Isolated Torrent Stack (gluetun + qBittorrent + Prowlarr + JDownloader2 + RDTClient + Aria2 + AriaNg)
Tdarr Server-Worker Architecture (server StatefulSet + worker Deployment + shared media)
Open WebUI SearXNG Web Search Pipeline

nodes.norm_label: repo layout — two deployment patterns coexist

nodes.label: Incident 001: Longhorn mount failures on k3s-node-3
nodes.norm_label: incident 001: longhorn mount failures on k3s-node-3

nodes.label: Tiered Storage Design (30d Longhorn hot â†’ 60d MinIO/NAS cold)
nodes.norm_label: tiered storage design (30d longhorn hot a†’ 60d minio/nas cold)

nodes.label: ArgoCD Multi-Source Pattern (Helm chart from upstream + values from git)
nodes.norm_label: argocd multi-source pattern (helm chart from upstream + values from git)

nodes.label: Sync Wave Ordering for Monitoring Stack
nodes.norm_label: sync wave ordering for monitoring stack

nodes.label: Monitoring Telemetry Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Minio (S3 backend) at sync-wave -2 â†’ kube-state-metrics/node-exporter (collectors) at -1 â†’ Loki/Mimir (storage) at 0 â†’ Alloy (agents) at 1 â†’ Grafana (visualization) at 2. Explicit sync-wave ordering enforces this dataflow dependency chain.

nodes.label: Helm Chart Multi-Source Deploy Pattern
nodes.rationale: 7 monitoring apps use ArgoCD multi-source: a helm chart from an upstream repo + inline values file from the local git repo. Separates chart version management from configuration.

nodes.label: arr-stack Namespace Co-location
nodes.rationale: tailscale (VPN networking) and tdarr (transcoding) deploy into the arr-stack namespace alongside the arr media management apps, sharing network and storage context.

nodes.label: Reverse-Proxy Auto-Discovery Pattern
nodes.rationale: ApplicationSet with git directory generator auto-discovers subdirectories under reverse-proxy/ and creates per-app ArgoCD Applications. No per-app manifest needed.

nodes.label: Longhorn distributed block storage
nodes.rationale: Default storage class with 2 replicas; used by most app PVCs for RWO config/state volumes

nodes.label: SMB CSI RWX shared storage
nodes.rationale: RWX media shares served from 192.168.0.75 via smb.csi.k8s.io; used by arrs, jellyfin, minio for bulk media/data that must be writable from any node

nodes.label: cert-manager ACME TLS automation
nodes.rationale: LetsEncrypt prod issuer via Cloudflare DNS01; wildcard cert *.techtronics.top in kube-system

nodes.label: MetalLB L2 load balancer
nodes.rationale: LAN IP pool 192.168.0.40-70 + IPv6 range; used by minecraft-lan LoadBalancer service

nodes.label: Co-located tightly-coupled containers
nodes.rationale: Multiple apps use a single Pod with sidecars sharing networking: arr-stack (6 containers), jellyfin (3), minecraft-server (2)

nodes.label: Recreate strategy for RWO volumes
nodes.rationale: Deployments using RWO PVCs use Recreate strategy to avoid RollingUpdate deadlock; seen in arrs, dashy, frostbite, jellyfin, minio

nodes.label: Tailscale kernel-mode sidecar
nodes.rationale: minecraft-server uses Tailscale sidecar in kernel TUN mode (TS_USERSPACE=false) with persistent state PVC for node identity

nodes.label: VPN Torr SMB Media PV+PVC 10Ti RWX
nodes.norm_label: vpn torr smb media pv+pvc 10ti rwx

nodes.label: RWO PVC + Recreate strategy deadlock avoidance
nodes.norm_label: rwo pvc + recreate strategy deadlock avoidance

nodes.label: Gluetun VPN sidecar provides network for torrent containers
nodes.norm_label: gluetun vpn sidecar provides network for torrent containers

nodes.label: Proxmox VE Node 2
nodes.rationale: Exposes Proxmox VE node 2 web UI at 192.168.0.212:8006 (HTTPS) via proxmox-2.techtronics.top. Uses Endpoints+Service+Ingress triple pattern with nginx backend-protocol:HTTPS and proxy-ssl-verify:false for self-signed certs.

nodes.label: Proxmox VE Node 3
nodes.rationale: Exposes Proxmox VE node 3 web UI at 192.168.0.213:8006 (HTTPS) via proxmox-3.techtronics.top.

nodes.label: Proxmox VE Node 4
nodes.rationale: Exposes Proxmox VE node 4 web UI at 192.168.0.214:8006 (HTTPS) via proxmox-4.techtronics.top.

nodes.label: Router Web Interface
nodes.rationale: Exposes router web UI at 192.168.0.1:80 (HTTP) via router.techtronics.top.

nodes.label: Speed Test Server
nodes.rationale: Exposes speed test service at 192.168.0.197:30003 (HTTP) via speed.techtronics.top. Uses proxy-buffering:off and proxy-body-size:100m for throughput testing.

nodes.label: TrueNAS 2 Web Interface
nodes.rationale: Exposes TrueNAS 2 web UI at 192.168.0.75:80 (HTTP) via truenas-2.techtronics.top.

nodes.label: Ubuntu Server 1
nodes.rationale: Exposes Ubuntu server 1 service at 192.168.0.237:8082 (HTTP) via ubuntu-server-1.techtronics.top.

nodes.label: Ubuntu Server 2
nodes.rationale: Exposes Ubuntu server 2 service at 192.168.0.197:8080 (HTTP) via ubuntu-server-2.techtronics.top.

nodes.label: Reverse Proxy External Service Pattern
nodes.rationale: Design pattern for exposing external (non-cluster) services through Kubernetes: a manually-created Endpoints resource points to an external IP:port, a headless-like Service (ClusterIP, no selector) references the Endpoints by name, and an Ingress with nginx ingressClassName exposes the service at a *.techtronics.top subdomain. Avoids per-app ArgoCD yaml â€” auto-discovered by reverse-proxy pattern.
